articles.title: Henry Patten's Scary Trip Home from Dubai
articles.summary: Henry Patten is a tennis player from Britain who was in Dubai for a tennis tournament. While he was there, there was a conflict in the Middle East, and Iran attacked the United Arab Emirates with missiles. This made it hard for Henry and other people to go home. He saw a drone hit a building near where he was staying. Henry had to stay in Dubai longer than planned and tried to drive to Oman, but it didn't work. Finally, he took a flight back to London, but he was very scared because he doesn't like flying. On the plane, he felt nervous, but he made it home safely. This experience made him decide not to play in another tennis tournament in Indian Wells.

articles.title: Dave's Big Concert in London
articles.summary: Dave, a famous British musician, performed a special concert at the London O2 Arena to celebrate 10 years in music. He played songs from his new album and old hits, and fans sang along and recorded videos. Dave thanked his fans and said he felt happy to be home in London. He talked to young fans, telling them they are powerful and have bright futures. The show had a band and a stage that let Dave get close to the audience, making it feel personal. It was a fun night celebrating his music journey.

articles.title: Swiss Vote on TV Fee Cut
articles.summary: Swiss people are voting this weekend to decide if they should pay less money each year for their national TV and radio. Right now, every household pays 335 Swiss francs, which is more than in Germany and Austria. A right-wing party wants to lower the fee to 200 francs and says it's too expensive when people are struggling with high costs. They think programs can be made cheaper today. But others disagree, saying the fee helps support shows in all four Swiss languages like German and French, which keeps the country united. They also worry that cutting the fee could mean fewer jobs and less news from around the world. The vote is expected to be close, especially as young people use streaming services more.

articles.title: Oil Prices Go Up After Qatar Warning
articles.summary: Oil prices have jumped to their highest in over two years because Qatar's energy minister warned that all oil and gas production in the Gulf might stop soon due to conflict in the Middle East. This region is important for global energy supplies. When oil prices rise, it affects many things like the cost of filling up cars, heating homes, and buying food. In the UK, petrol and diesel prices have already gone up. Experts say if the conflict continues, it could hurt the world's economy by making energy more expensive and causing shortages. This situation is being watched closely to see if it becomes a bigger crisis.

articles.title: Tennis Player Gets Scary Messages
articles.summary: Panna Udvardy is a tennis player from Hungary. She got scary messages on her phone from someone she didn't know. The messages said her family would be hurt if she didn't lose her tennis match. They sent pictures of her family and a gun. This made her very scared. Police came to protect her and her family during her match in Turkey. She lost the match to another player. Udvardy says this is not okay and no player should have to deal with this. Other tennis players have also gotten threats like this. The tennis organization is looking into it to find out who sent the messages and how they got personal information.

articles.title: Meghan's Brand and Netflix Stop Working Together
articles.summary: Meghan, the Duchess of Sussex, has a lifestyle brand called As ever that makes things like jam and cookies. Last year, Netflix helped pay for the brand to start. Now, they have decided to end their partnership. This means As ever will be on its own without Netflix's help. Netflix said they are happy they could help Meghan's brand grow. Meghan will keep running As ever by herself. This is different from her TV shows with Netflix, which did not do very well. The brand is now ready to be independent and continue making products.
